37a80c0ad9e3.title: Lecture 1
37a80c0ad9e3.notes_text: Year 3 Semester II 2025/2026. CSC101 Enterprise Systems lecture one introduces enterprise resource planning. It explains how registration, finance, and teaching workflows stay connected in one system. Students should identify the major modules and the data each module shares.

1b1495c313dc.title: Lecture 2
1b1495c313dc.notes_text: Year 3 Semester II 2025/2026. CSC101 Enterprise Systems lecture two focuses on workflow mapping. The note compares student registration, lecturer uploads, assessment records, and announcements. Learners should follow each workflow from data entry to reporting.

7125a49b1e96.title: Lecture 3
7125a49b1e96.notes_text: Year 3 Semester II 2025/2026. CSC101 Enterprise Systems lecture three covers dashboards, reports, and service support. It shows how indexed lecture notes improve quiz generation and chatbot guidance. Students should summarize how academic support data is presented to end users.

f6a18ade2280.title: Lecture 1
f6a18ade2280.notes_text: Year 2 Semester II 2025/2026. BIT220 Database Design lecture one introduces entities, attributes, and relationships. It uses university records such as students, courses, fees, and results as examples. Students should practice separating transactional data from reference data.

e0379909b912.title: Lecture 2
e0379909b912.notes_text: Year 2 Semester II 2025/2026. BIT220 Database Design lecture two explains first, second, and third normal form. The examples use registration tables and lecturer resource indexes. Learners should identify duplicated fields and redesign them into cleaner structures.

5339cb1d2de3.title: Lecture 3
5339cb1d2de3.notes_text: Year 2 Semester II 2025/2026. BIT220 Database Design lecture three demonstrates queries for results, announcements, and timetable views. It also links database design to search and retrieval support in course systems. Students should write sample queries that answer common academic questions.

db2dbcca1076.title: Lecture 1
db2dbcca1076.notes_text: Year 3 Semester II 2025/2026. BIS210 Systems Analysis lecture one introduces systems analysis in an academic portal setting. It reviews actors, goals, inputs, and outputs for students, lecturers, and administrators. Students should identify the core business processes supported by the portal.

a9596f208984.title: Lecture 2
a9596f208984.notes_text: Year 3 Semester II 2025/2026. BIS210 Systems Analysis lecture two captures requirements through interviews, observations, and use cases. The lecture note models login, resource access, translation support, and lecturer feedback. Learners should turn user goals into clear functional requirements.

1252ec7601d7.title: Lecture 3
1252ec7601d7.notes_text: Year 3 Semester II 2025/2026. BIS210 Systems Analysis lecture three focuses on process flows, exceptions, and user journeys. It explains how a student opens a course unit, reads lecture PDFs, and asks for help from the chatbot. Students should diagram the flow from course selection to resource review.
